message:
fix(assert): bound pointer scans in __assert_perror_fail (bd-9bijw)

__assert_fail uses scan_c_string(ptr, known_remaining(ptr as usize))
to bound every incoming string pointer and substitutes "??" when the
pointer is null or the string is not terminated within the known
region. __assert_perror_fail used CStr::from_ptr directly, letting an
adversarial caller with a non-NUL-terminated pointer walk arbitrary
process memory — an info-leak via the stderr diagnostic and a crash
vector when the walk crosses an unmapped page.

Fix: share the bounded-read pattern via a local read_bounded helper
that matches __assert_fail's behavior. Abort-path entrypoints cannot
be tested in-process (same convention as the fortify __chk_fail
tests), so the change is covered by structural parity with the
already-validated __assert_fail hardening.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-747","title":"Stub wave 1: getenv/setenv/unsetenv + environment invariants","description":"Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- Implement getenv/setenv/unsetenv semantics with thread-safety policy and strict/hardened parity notes.
- Add fixture tests for overwrite flags, invalid names, and concurrent reads.

Acceptance:
- Environment fixtures match glibc behavior for covered cases.
- Symbols removed from top-stub list and support matrix upgraded accordingly.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.830918180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","env","stubs"]}

 {"id":"bd-7cba","title":"bd-3bg subtask: Minimal codec scope/exclusion ledger + compatibility intent","description":"Background:
- iconv scope must be explicit to avoid overpromising full iconvdata parity before deterministic generation pipeline exists.

Goal:
- Define minimal codec scope, exclusions, and compatibility intent for phase progression.

Deliverables:
1) Codec inclusion/exclusion ledger with rationale.
2) Compatibility goals per included codec family.
3) Support matrix mapping for supported/unsupported codecs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Scope is explicit and user-facing claims are accurate.
- No ambiguous codec support status.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for scope manifest parsing/validation.
- Structured logs for scope checks and drift detections.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyMink","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.290717538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T04:41:23.346392084Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T04:41:23.346371766Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic iconv codec scope ledger drift gate, structured evidence logs, and CI integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","iconv","locale","spec","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1057,"issue_id":"bd-7cba","author":"IndigoMaple","text":"Progress update: implemented iconv codec scope/exclusion ledger and drift gates.

Changes:
- Added tests/conformance/iconv_codec_scope_ledger.v1.json (included/excluded codec ledger + rationale + support-matrix mapping).
- Added crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/iconv_codec_scope_ledger_test.rs (schema, set consistency, exclusion rationale, support-matrix alignment checks).
- Updated crates/frankenlibc-core/src/iconv/mod.rs with explicit phase-1 codec constants + normalization/out-of-scope tests.
- Updated support_matrix.json iconv strict semantics to include UTF-32.
- Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md iconv hard-parts entry to reference ledger artifact.

Validation:
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (pass)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core iconv_open_rejects_out_of_scope_codecs -- --nocapture (pass)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test iconv_codec_scope_ledger_test -- --nocapture (pass)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (fails on pre-existing unrelated frankenlibc-abi lints/warnings)
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (fails due pre-existing formatting diffs in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/grp_abi.rs and crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pwd_abi.rs).","created_at":"2026-02-15T19:28:09Z"},{"id":1058,"issue_id":"bd-7cba","author":"HazyMink","text":"Progress update (2026-02-26): added deterministic iconv scope-ledger drift gate and wired it into extended CI.\n\nChanges:\n- Added scripts/check_iconv_codec_scope_ledger.sh (bd-7cba gate).\n- Gate validates ledger schema/identity, included+excluded codec sets vs core iconv phase-1 constants, alias normalization coverage, and support_matrix semantics alignment for iconv/iconv_open/iconv_close.\n- Gate emits structured evidence artifacts: target/conformance/iconv_codec_scope_ledger.report.json and target/conformance/iconv_codec_scope_ledger.log.jsonl plus tests/cve_arena/results/bd-7cba/{trace.jsonl,artifact_index.json}.\n- Wired gate into scripts/ci.sh extended gates after iconv table generation gate.\n\nValidation:\n- scripts/check_iconv_codec_scope_ledger.sh -> PASS\n- rch check -> PASS (8/8 workers healthy)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test iconv_codec_scope_ledger_test -> PASS (4 passed)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core iconv_open_rejects_out_of_scope_codecs -> PASS (1 passed)\n\nNote: observed unrelated pre-existing warnings in frankenlibc-abi/src/stdbit_abi.rs during test build output; no changes made in that surface.","created_at":"2026-02-26T04:41:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9b0b","title":"syscall: sched_setscheduler negative pid with null param should still return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for sched_setscheduler(-1, SCHED_OTHER, NULL), so the invalid-pid contract dominates a null param pointer. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:01:46.569234797Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T02:40:02.580319009Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T02:40:02.578150523Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-9bijw","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] __assert_perror_fail walks unbounded memory via CStr::from_ptr, inconsistent with __assert_fail hardening","description":"Location: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs:3248-3257 (__assert_perror_fail).

__assert_fail (line 3197) carefully uses scan_c_string(ptr, known_remaining(ptr as usize)) with a defined-length bound on every string pointer (assertion, file, function) and substitutes '??' when the bound can't be satisfied. __assert_perror_fail does the opposite:
    let f = if file.is_null() { c"??" } else { unsafe { CStr::from_ptr(file) } };
    let func = if function.is_null() { c"??" } else { unsafe { CStr::from_ptr(function) } };
CStr::from_ptr walks bytes until it encounters a NUL — with no bound. An adversarial or corrupted caller passing a non-NUL-terminated pointer walks unbounded process memory. Consequences:
- Information leak: arbitrary process memory bytes flushed through the error message to stderr.
- Segfault: if the walk crosses an unmapped-page boundary.

Reachability: exported libc surface. Any assert_perror() call made from untrusted/foreign code, or any plain call to __assert_perror_fail with crafted pointers, triggers this. In practice __FILE__/__FUNCTION__ are always NUL-terminated string literals, but the libc boundary does not enforce that — the compiler guarantee does not cover hostile callers.

Severity: HIGH (info leak / segfault reachable from a single libc entrypoint), though mitigated in practice by macro-expanded call sites that happen to use string literals.

Fix plan:
- Mirror __assert_fail's hardening: scan_c_string with known_remaining(ptr as usize), substitute '??' when the string is null OR not terminated within the known-remaining region.
- Add a regression test using a non-null-terminated buffer — prior to the fix this would segfault or leak neighboring bytes; after the fix it must substitute '??' cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:16:47.678923032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:17:57.812087648Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:17:57.808924677Z","close_reason":"Fixed: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs __assert_perror_fail now mirrors __assert_fail's hardening — both file and function pointers are read via scan_c_string(ptr, known_remaining(ptr as usize)) and substituted with '??' when null or not terminated within the known region. Prior behavior used CStr::from_ptr which walks memory until an accidental NUL is hit, letting an adversarial caller either leak bytes into the stderr message or crash on an unmapped page. Abort-path entrypoints cannot be tested in-process (per the existing fortify_abi_test.rs convention: 'The abort paths (__chk_fail) cannot be tested in-process since they call abort()'). Verified by rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_review cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -> clean in 3.36s. The fix re-uses the already-tested read-bounded pattern from __assert_fail at lines 3198-3232.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9bixy","title":"[fuzz-harness] fuzz_mmap: Op::Protect gaining PROT_WRITE didn't re-stamp guard bytes; cleanup check_guards false-positives","description":"Found and fixed in this solo session via systematic deep-fuzz sweeps. Fix shipped in 71ffa897. Filing retroactively per Tick-95 request to track each harness defect explicitly. Not a library bug — the frankenlibc ABI was correct throughout; only the fuzz harness invariants needed tightening.","notes":"Fixed in 71ffa897 — re-stamp guards when Protect transitions !PROT_WRITE → PROT_WRITE.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:29:50.179107Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:36:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:36:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.10","title":"stdio: export _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_ aliases bound to NativeFile storage","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §3 (final paragraph). Some statically-linked-against-libc binaries resolve _IO_2_1_stdin_, _IO_2_1_stdout_, _IO_2_1_stderr_ directly via dlsym or link-time resolution, bypassing the public stdin/stdout/stderr aliases. Without exporting these names from FrankenLibC, the binary sees two distinct streams (one native FrankenLibC, one host glibc) and the program misbehaves subtly: partial flushes, lost output, double-close.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile layout)

## What to do

1. Add the three symbol names to crates/frankenlibc-abi/version_scripts/libc.map under the GLIBC_2.2.5 version tag.
2. In stdio_abi.rs (or io_internal_abi.rs), declare the underscored names as #[no_mangle] static aliases pointing to the *same* NativeFile storage as the public stdin/stdout/stderr globals. Do NOT allocate a second set of NativeFile objects — they must be the same object so a flush via stdin reaches the same buffer as a flush via _IO_2_1_stdin_.
3. Verify the alias by building the .so and inspecting nm: nm -D target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so | grep _IO_2_1 must show the three symbols at the same address as stdin/stdout/stderr.
4. Add an integration test: build a small C program that takes both stdin and _IO_2_1_stdin_, asserts they are equal, and runs it under LD_PRELOAD.

## Done

- libc.map exports the three aliases
- nm verification passes
- Integration test passes
- The aliases survive a release build with strip+lto","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:28:41.061649453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:44:25.584876183Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:44:25.582645349Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","stdio","version-script"]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.15","title":"stdio: build NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T vtable + 17 trampolines for indirect dispatch","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §10. Many glibc-built binaries (especially with -fno-plt) call vfprintf indirectly through ((struct _IO_FILE_plus *)fp)->vtable->__xsputn(...) rather than through the PLT-resolved vfprintf symbol. If our vtable pointer is null or wrong, the program crashes inside __xsputn.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile must already have a vtable slot)

## What to do

1. In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs, define repr(C) struct _IO_jump_t with the 17 standard slots: __dummy, __dummy2, __finish, __overflow, __underflow, __uflow, __pbackfail, __xsputn, __xsgetn, __seekoff, __seekpos, __setbuf, __sync, __doallocate, __read, __write, __seek, __close, __stat, __showmanyc, __imbue. Field offsets pinned to glibc 2.34.
2. Create a static NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T: _IO_jump_t = ... initialized with extern "C" function pointers to Rust trampolines.
3. Each trampoline takes a *mut FILE, casts to *mut NativeFile, acquires the recursive lock, and dispatches to the native kernel function used by the public ABI entrypoint. The trampolines for __xsputn and __xsgetn are the high-volume ones; benchmark them.
4. Every NativeFile constructor (bd-9chy.5..10) sets `self.vtable = &NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T as *const _ as *mut _`.
5. Trampoline must handle the foreign-pointer-adoption case: if the FILE* it receives has the bloom precheck saying "not ours," route through adopt_foreign_file (separate bead bd-9chy.16). For now, panic with a clear message; the next bead wires real adoption.
6. Unit test: build a small Rust test that takes &mut NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T.__xsputn, calls it with a NativeFile* and verifies bytes are written.

## Done

- _IO_jump_t struct defined with offsets matching glibc
- NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T static built and wired into NativeFile constructors
- All 17 trampolines have implementations (some can be no-ops or default behavior)
- Unit test for indirect __xsputn dispatch passes
- Snapshot test tests/runtime_math/golden/io_jump_dispatch.v1.json captures vtable-vs-PLT call counts on a representative workload","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:03.906540102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T17:38:04.026649614Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T17:38:04.026388970Z","close_reason":"Implemented _IO_jump_t vtable with 21 trampolines for indirect dispatch. All slots wired to native stdio functions. NativeFile::new() sets vtable to NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T. Layout tests added. Also fixed TLS corruption and type mismatches.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stdio","vtable"]}
@@ -722,7 +723,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-hzkc6","title":"syscall: signalfd4 existing fd update with same mask should return same fd without touching errno","description":"Host glibc returns the same fd and leaves errno unchanged for signalfd(existing_fd, &mask, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the in-place update contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:56:03.717758642Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.635816783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.633658460Z","close_reason":"signalfd4(existing_fd, same_mask, 0) → same_fd (in-place update) pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-i3om4","title":"[HIGH] runtime_policy::ffi_pcc tests leak global state across parallel test threads","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails with:

```
thread 'runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:2168
test result: FAILED. 170 passed; 1 failed
```

Running the same test in isolation (`cargo test ... ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture`) passes cleanly: '1 passed; 0 failed; 170 filtered out'.

## Diagnosis

The test already acquires `ffi_pcc_lock()` and calls `reset_ffi_pcc_state_for_tests`, `reset_decision_contract_machine_for_tests`, `enable_runtime_kernel_for_tests`, `set_mode_state_for_tests(MODE_HARDENED)`. That pattern suggests the author was aware of test-global-state coupling. The fact that the assertion at line 2168 (`assert_eq!(decision.policy_id, FFI_PCC_POLICY_BASE + 10)`) fires only under concurrent test scheduling means **at least one of those resets does not cover all mutable state that another test modifies concurrently** — either a static/lazy cache, a thread-local, or a singleton that is not inside the lock's scope.

Adjacent tests in the same module (`ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_snprintf` et al.) use the same lock and reset helpers and presumably race on the same shared state.

## Fix direction

1. Grep for every `static ATOMIC` / `LazyLock` / `thread_local` / `Mutex<` in runtime_policy.rs that the certificate gate path touches. Classify which are covered by `ffi_pcc_lock` vs `decision_contract_machine` vs neither.
2. Any state touched by `decide()` along the `lookup_active_ffi_pcc_certificate` -> `ffi_pcc_decision` path that is not inside one of the reset-helpers is a candidate leak.
3. Confirmed repro: run the full `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` twice in a row; the failure is sometimes the 'memcpy' test, sometimes 'snprintf' or another adjacent one — classic test-ordering-dependent leak.

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace`. Part of the same full-review batch as bd-g2bd5 (glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption). Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture` passes (1/1).
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails at the same test (170/171).

 {"id":"bd-ibh7","title":"Divergent ABI source scanning between build.rs and check_replacement_guard.sh breaks standalone build","description":"`crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` scans all `.rs` files in `src/` for `libc::` callthroughs, which catches `host_resolve.rs:51 libc::dlvsym` and breaks the `standalone` build. However, `scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh` only scans `*_abi.rs` files, missing `host_resolve.rs` entirely. Furthermore, `build.rs` does not implement the newly added `source_scan_exceptions` from `replacement_profile.json`.\n\nTo fix:\n1. Make `check_replacement_guard.sh` scan all `.rs` files (or align the scopes).\n2.  {"id":"bd-id3","title":"Verification matrix: required unit/e2e/log artifacts for every critique bead","description":"Goal: make verification obligations explicit in plan space before implementation.

- Machine-readable matrix keyed by bead ID with required evidence fields:

  - structured log artifacts (schema + sample)
  - perf/isomorphism evidence where applicable
- Coverage status dashboard (missing/partial/complete).

Acceptance:
- Every open critique bead has a populated verification row.
- Missing evidence blocks bead closure.

- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).

 {"id":"bd-o4yda","title":"fuzz: open/openat argument validation (path + flags + mode)","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_open_syscalls.rs targeting frankenlibc_abi::fcntl_abi::{open,openat,creat} ABI wrappers — the NARROWEST input boundary where the libc replacement validates pathname pointers, O_* flag combinations, and mode_t.

## Archetype (per /testing-fuzzing)
1 + 3 combined: Crash-detector on our impl, plus **differential** against libc::open/openat for inputs that are safe to call on a disposable tmp path.

## Inputs
struct OpenFuzzInput {
  path: Vec<u8>,          // mutated from seeds: /tmp/<ok>, /dev/null, /nonexistent, ..\X, empty, embedded NUL (rejected by CString)
  flags: i32,             // O_CREAT|O_RDWR|... ANDed against the known-valid mask
  mode: u32,              // 0..=0o7777
  use_openat: bool,
  at_fd: i32,             // AT_FDCWD or a cached tmpdir fd
}

## Invariants
- Never panic on any input
- rc is either a non-negative fd OR -1 with errno in the documented set (EACCES/ENOENT/EEXIST/EISDIR/ENOTDIR/EFAULT/EINVAL/ELOOP/ENAMETOOLONG/ENOSPC/EROFS/EMFILE/ENFILE/EPERM/ENOMEM)
- For any successful open, our fd close() returns 0
- Host parity: our rc/errno family matches libc::openat when the path refers to an inert location (/tmp/<random> or /dev/null)

## Expected bug classes
- Missing flag-bit validation, O_TMPFILE misparse, O_PATH ABI gap, pathname length truncation, errno leak between calls, FD leak under error branches, membrane decision divergence for boundary addresses.

## Cleanup contract
Every successful fd must be closed before iteration ends to avoid fd exhaustion; use a per-iteration tmpdir with restoreable unlinking.

## Score (fault × independence × cost)
5 × 5 × /2 ≈ 12.5 — highest-priority fuzz target after pattern_match.

Per skill: register in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml, verify cargo +nightly fuzz run links (bd-0ubkr now closed), enforce ≥1000 exec/s (may need tmpfs for fd churn).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:20.475456710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:13:08.446218796Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o4yda","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:42.908929016Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-oai","title":"Epic: Dual-Mode Semantics & Deterministic Replay","description":"Dual-mode execution: Strict (default, ABI-compatible, no repair, <20ns) and Hardened (deterministic repair per policy tables, structured JSON evidence per repair, <200ns). Mode selected immutably at process startup for deterministic replay. RuntimeKernelSnapshot (154-field state capture). Evidence symbol records with RaptorQ-inspired redundancy.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Session 30: Epic closed — all children completed

All child beads closed:
- bd-oai.1: Dual-mode selection + strict/hardened execution paths ✅
- bd-oai.2: RuntimeKernelSnapshot (165+ fields) + deterministic replay ✅  
- bd-oai.3: Evidence symbol records + RaptorQ-inspired redundancy ✅

Verification:
- 16 E2E tests in runtime_math_dual_mode_e2e_test.rs (all pass)
- 33 evidence system tests (all pass)
- Deterministic replay produces identical decision streams and JSONL logs
- Branch-diversity enforcement: Healthy/NearViolation/Violation states verified
- Golden snapshot regression gate: JSON equality under deterministic inputs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:48.791246202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T05:13:40.030481360Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T05:13:32Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","dual-mode","epic","frankenlibc","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oai","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1124,"issue_id":"bd-oai","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-oai.1","title":"Dual-Mode: Mode selection at process startup (immutable, deterministic)","description":"Implement mode selection: Strict vs Hardened chosen at process startup (env var / config), immutable thereafter. Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:54.495718406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:03:43.325060776Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:03:43.325029658Z","close_reason":"Thread-local immutable mode caching and deterministic startup selection completed; verification evidence already logged in comment #455.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dual-mode","frankenlibc","startup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oai.1","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1125,"issue_id":"bd-oai.1","author":"MagentaFalcon","text":"2026-02-25 completion pass (MagentaFalcon): implemented thread-local runtime mode caching in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs for zero-cost hot-path mode reads after first resolution, while preserving process-immutable mode semantics. Added helper wiring: load/store/clear thread-local cache; integrated caching into resolved/race/startup branches; preserved strict fallback behavior for unresolved/reentrant paths. Updated tests: ModeStateGuard now snapshots/restores TLS mode cache; cache_reset_reparses_mode_from_environment clears TLS cache; added mode_populates_thread_local_cache_after_resolution. Verification (all heavy commands via rch): rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets (pass); rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi mode_ -- --nocapture (pass; includes new mode_populates_thread_local_cache_after_resolution + existing mode immutability/logging tests).  {"id":"bd-sq7ae","title":"atfork runners hold Mutex across user callbacks — self-deadlock on re-entrant pthread_atfork","description":"run_atfork_prepare/parent/child in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:4350-4385 hold ATFORK_HANDLERS.lock() across arbitrary user-callback invocation. A prepare/parent/child handler that transitively calls pthread_atfork (common in lazy-init library paths) re-locks a non-recursive std::sync::Mutex on the same thread -> deadlock. Concrete interleaving: T1 fork() -> run_atfork_prepare() holds lock -> calls H1 -> H1 init'd some library L2 that registers its own handler via pthread_atfork -> pthread_atfork tries ATFORK_HANDLERS.lock() -> self-deadlock. Class 1 (classic mutex deadlock). Fix: snapshot handlers (Vec<AtforkHandlers> with derive(Clone, Copy)), drop guard, iterate snapshot. Affects: unistd_abi.rs:5415/5432/5434 fork interposer and process_abi.rs:139/156/159 fork interposer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:26:44.711167663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:40:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:40:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: snapshot ATFORK_HANDLERS under lock, iterate snapshot unlocked. Regression test added in tests/pthread_abi_test.rs. Landed in 437e3d0d.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-sttk9","title":"fuzz: read/write/pread/pwrite buffer + count validation","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_read_write.rs targeting frankenlibc_abi::unistd_abi::{read,write,pread,pwrite,readv,writev}. Focus on buffer-bound validation + short-read/short-write semantics + errno correctness for invalid fd/len/buf.

## Archetype
1 + 3: Crash-detector + **differential** against libc for matched calls, using a seeded pipe pair and /dev/zero / /dev/null fds as deterministic backends.

## Inputs
struct ReadWriteFuzzInput {
  op: u8,                 // 0=read, 1=write, 2=pread, 3=pwrite, 4=readv, 5=writev
  fd_selector: u8,        // 0=/dev/zero, 1=/dev/null, 2=pipe-read-end, 3=pipe-write-end, 4=closed-fd, 5=-1
  buf_len: u16,           // 0..=32768
  buf_offset: u8,         // shift within a larger allocation (exercises decide's address checks)
  poison_ptr: bool,       // NULL or address we can catch
  offset: i64,            // for pread/pwrite
  iovec_count: u8,        // 0..=8 for readv/writev
}

## Invariants
- No panic on any input
- rc is a non-negative byte count OR -1 with errno in {EBADF, EFAULT, EINVAL, EIO, EAGAIN, EINTR, EPIPE, ENOSPC, ESPIPE, EOVERFLOW}
- Host parity for the /dev/zero, /dev/null, closed-fd cases: ours matches libc::read/write bit-for-bit
- readv/writev total byte count equals the sum of per-iovec byte counts
- Buffer is not read/written past requested len (ASAN + explicit sentinel check before and after)

## Expected bug classes
- Off-by-one in iovec loop, iov_len overflow, fd validation bypass, membrane decision mismatches for cross-page buffers, missing EINTR retry, sign handling for large counts (SSIZE_MAX), ESPIPE not mapped for pread/pwrite on pipes

## Sentinel pattern
Allocate 2*buf_len + 2 pages, place guard bytes (0xAA) before and after the requested window; after the call assert guard bytes unchanged.

## Score
5 × 5 × /2 ≈ 12.5 — tied with bd-o4yda for priority.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:39.154205881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:22.578845312Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:22.576538966Z","close_reason":"fuzz_read_write harness committed and green: 16.3M execs / 108K exec/s in 151s. Covers read, write, pread, pwrite, readv, writev against 5 deterministic fd backends (/dev/zero, /dev/null, pipe read, pipe write, closed fd, wild fd). Sentinel-byte guards before/after every buffer window catch any over-count write. Differential parity against libc for non-pipe backends. Two harness-side bugs fixed during bring-up (count > buf_size overrun, buf_offset into post-pad). Zero real impl bugs under the invariant set — the read/write wrapper is solid. Committed 4e263808.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sttk9","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:44.052743346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
